Overview.content: The API layer is the external surface of the Multi-Agent IDE. REST controllers accept HTTP requests and translate them into EventBus publications, service calls, or direct agent pipeline invocations. The CLI bypasses HTTP entirely — it calls the same controller methods in-process. Every mutating endpoint follows a fire-and-forget pattern: publish an event, return an `actionId`, and let the event bus drive execution.

Goal Execution Flow.content:
```
POST /api/orchestrator/start  (StartGoalRequest: goal, repositoryUrl, baseBranch, title, tags)
 │
 ├─ Creates root ArtifactKey (ak:ulid)
 ├─ Returns nodeId immediately
 │
 └─ @Async GoalExecutor.executeGoal()
      ├─ Validates repo path exists
      ├─ Normalizes repositoryUrl
      ├─ Publishes GoalStartedEvent
      └─ AgentLifecycleHandler.initializeOrchestrator(...)
           ├─ Creates OrchestratorNode
           ├─ Emits NodeAddedEvent + WorktreeCreatedEvent
           └─ AgentEventListener catches NodeAddedEvent → AgentRunner → blocks until workflow completes
```

The client then polls via SSE (`GET /api/events/stream`) or checks activity (`POST /api/ui/activity-check`).

Controller Endpoint Map.content:
| Endpoint group | Key operations | Target service |
|---|---|---|
| `/api/orchestrator` | `POST /start` (goal), `GET /onboarding/runs` | `GoalExecutor`, `OnboardingOrchestrationService` |
| `/api/agents` | `POST /pause`, `/stop`, `/resume`, `/prune`, `/branch`, `/review-request` | `AgentControlService` → EventBus |
| `/api/interrupts` | `GET /pending`, `POST /` (request), `POST /resolve`, `POST /detail` | `PermissionGate`, `AgentControlService`, `EventStreamRepository` |
| `/api/permissions` | `GET /pending`, `POST /resolve`, `GET /detail` | `PermissionGate` (ALLOW_ONCE/ALWAYS, REJECT_ONCE/ALWAYS) |
| `/api/agent-conversations` | `POST /respond`, `POST /list` | `AgentExecutor.controllerResponseExecution()` |
| `/api/ui` | `POST /feedback`, `POST /diff/revert`, `POST /message`, `POST /activity-check` | `UiStateService`, EventBus |
| `/api/events` | `GET /stream` | `SseEventAdapter` (SSE registration + catch-up replay) |
| `/api/runs` | `POST /start`, `GET /`, `POST /timeline`, `POST /actions`, `POST /persistence-validation` | `DebugRunQueryService`, `DebugRunPersistenceValidationService` |

Agent Control: Fire-and-Forget via Event.content:
All agent control operations (`/api/agents/*`) follow the same pattern:

```
POST /api/agents/pause  →  AgentControlService.pauseAgent(nodeId)
                              → eventBus.publish(PauseEvent(nodeId))
                              → return { actionId: UUID, status: "queued" }
```

The EventBus delivers the event to `AgentEventListener`, which routes it to `AgentRunner`. The REST call returns immediately — the caller monitors progress via SSE.

Agent-to-Controller Conversation Flow.content:
When an agent calls `call_controller`, it blocks on an interrupt. The controller responds via:

```
POST /api/agent-conversations/respond  (interruptId, message, checklistAction?, expectResponse?)
 │
 ▼
AgentExecutor.controllerResponseExecution()
 ├─ Derives key hierarchy from interruptId (itself a child ArtifactKey)
 ├─ Resolves OperationContext from the agent's live execution state
 ├─ Retrieves original AgentToControllerRequest from BlackboardHistory
 ├─ Renders response through full decoration/template pipeline
 ├─ permissionGate.resolveInterrupt() with rendered text
 └─ Emits AgentCallEvent(RETURNED)
```

CLI: In-Process Controller Call.content:
The CLI is Spring Shell, profile-gated to `@Profile("cli")`. The `tui` command launches `TuiSession`. When the user submits a goal in TUI:

```
TuiSession.ChatInputSubmitted
 → CliTuiRunner.startGoal(repo, goal)
   → OrchestrationController.startGoalAsync(new StartGoalRequest(...))
```

This is a direct in-process method call — same code path as the REST layer, no HTTP involved.

Configuration.content:
**`LlmModelSelectionProperties`** (`prefix: llm.model-selection`): Resolution priority: `byTemplate` > `byAgentType` > `defaultModel`. Each entry is `{ model, provider }` where provider maps to an `AcpProvider` enum.

**`ArtifactConfig`**: `@PostConstruct` subscribes `ArtifactEventListener` to the EventBus.

**`MultiAgentIdeConfig`**: `@ComponentScan` pulls in `AgUiSerdes`, `McpProperties`, `AcpChatModel`, `EnvConfigProps`.
